arts.ttl: Arsenal told Gary Neville, Jamie Carragher "wouldn't have stopped" if Gunners behaved in Liverpool manner
arts.sum: Gary Neville and Jamie Carragher would have ripped Arsenal to shreds had they behaved like Liverpool in the Merseyside derby, Charles Watts tells Sports Mole.

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy in plea to Leicester City protesters against Arsenal
arts.sum: Ruud van Nistelrooy is pleased that Leicester City protesters have the right to voice their frustrations but hopes they throw their full support behind the players once the whistle is blown against Arsenal. In response, van Nistelrooy said he is pleased to live in a country where protests are possible.

arts.ttl: Max Dowman: How Arsenal could register record-breaking teenager amid injury crisis
arts.sum: Arsenal are reportedly exploring a way to register 15-year-old Max Dowman to their Premier League squad.

arts.ttl: 'Liverpool lost their heads' - National media send Arsenal title race hope after Everton chaos - football.london
arts.sum: "In the context of the Premier League title race a draw is hardly a disaster for Liverpool – although the last-gasp manner of being denied the win, again, will hurt – as they extended their lead over Arsenal to seven points with 14 games to go," the Telegraph's chief football correspondent Jason Burt wrote . Burt's colleague Chris Bascombe, their Merseyside football reporter, also wrote : "From the perspective of the title race, Liverpool having to accept a seven-point rather than nine or even 11-point gap at the summit represents a well-delivered jab rather than a brutal left hook to their ambitions, albeit there will be many thousands of Arsenal fans who would willingly have joined the euphoric Everton fans on the pitch after Tarkowski’s equaliser."

arts.ttl: Arsenal got it right with £50m double striker transfer despite Havertz blow
arts.sum: Arsenal's injury crisis has also led to some people look at the Gunners' decision to sell Eddie Nketiah last summer to Crystal Palace and Folarin Balogun to Monaco in August 2023. Mikel Arteta is set to be without at least four first-team attackers for the foreseeable future, including Saturday's trip to Leicester City.

arts.ttl: Arsenal injury news after Havertz statement issued as Arteta update imminent
arts.sum: A club statement released on Thursday afternoon reads : "We can confirm that Kai Havertz has sustained a hamstring injury during a training session in Dubai last week. Benjamin White could, though, be back in the squad this weekend after returning to training with the team following knee surgery.

arts.ttl: Wonderkid Max Dowman banned from Arsenal debut amid Kai Havertz injury due to Premier League rule
arts.sum: He has one goal and two assists in six appearances for Arsenal's U18s and has regularly trained with the first team this season as he looks to make an impact at seniro level in the years to come. Labelled the "next Kaka", the recently turned 15-year-old has been part of first team training at London Colney a number of times already this season.

arts.ttl: The 16 games Kai Havertz will miss after Arsenal confirm injury blow as statement made
arts.sum: With the Germany international now out for the remainder of the season, he is set to miss at least 16 matches for the club - 14 of those fixtures will be played in the Premier League including the pivotal trip to Liverpool as well as clashes with Manchester United, Chelsea and Newcastle. Arsenal are preparing to return to Premier League action without their top forward Kai Havertz after a disastrous injury blow during the club's warm weather training camp in Dubai.

arts.ttl: “I was surprised’: Expert can’t believe Arsenal didn’t sign £30m player in January
arts.sum: Arsenal didn’t sign a new striker in the January transfer window, and that decision has already come back to bite the Gunners. Indeed, Kai Havertz...

arts.ttl: Kai Havertz out for the season as Arsenal confirm hamstring injury
arts.sum: The 25-year-old suffered the injury in Dubai last week.
